description_long: This is a tall, four-sided tapered bollard with a square base, constructed primarily from a dark-colored material that appears to be metal or reinforced plastic. Each face of the bollard displays bold white Japanese text and a red circular sign with a bicycle icon crossed out, indicating a prohibition on bicycle parking. The base is broad and flat, made to provide stability, and is mounted on a textured, stone-like platform. The surface of the bollard is matte with some visible wear, suggesting it is designed for outdoor urban environments. The top of the bollard is pointed, forming a narrow peak, and there are no visible moving or interactive parts.
description: A tall, tapered, four-sided bollard with Japanese no-bicycle-parking signs on each face, mounted on a square stone-like base. The surface is matte and dark, with a pointed top and bold white text.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Japanese text and no-bicycle sign clearly, with the square base and pointed top visible.
description_view_1: Side view with similar signage and text, showing the tapering shape and the broad, stable base.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with clear signage and the same structural features.
description_view_3: Angled view highlighting the tapering sides, pointed top, and the stone-like mounting platform.
